metadata.title: Catch a Package Hallucination Before It Becomes a Slopsquatting Attack

beats.narration_text: This is Musinique. Claude imports requests_oauth_helper — a name that doesn't exist on PyPI today but might be squatted tomorrow with malicious code inside.

props.command: Catch a Package Hallucination Before It Becomes a Slopsquatting Attack
props.segment: Catch a Package Hallucination Before It Becomes a Slopsquatting Attack

beats.narration_text: Hallucinated package names appear across thousands of model outputs. Slopsquatting works by registering those names with malicious payloads. The scanner catches the risk at the import line before pip install runs.

The same scaffolding principle that keeps a student from outsourcing their thinking protects a musician from outsourcing their voice. When Claude drafts, you judge. When Claude suggests a pitch angle, you decide if it's true. The tool that flags 'this is AI doing your creative work for you' is the tool that keeps your music yours.

artifactLines: The same tools the platforms use — pointed back at you.
Roll your own. Own the deck.

beats.narration_text: We ask Claude to write a package auditor that reads import statements from a file and checks each against the PyPI JSON API.

props.command:
claude "Write package_auditor.py:
  read Python file -> extract imports
  query PyPI JSON API per package
  report EXISTS / HALLUCINATED
  flag 404 as slopsquatting risk"

beats.narration_text: Claude writes the auditor using requests and the PyPI API. It extracts top-level package names only, handles both 'import X' and 'from X import Y', and prints a color-coded verdict per package.

props.title: package_auditor.py

beats.narration_text: Scanner run: requests — EXISTS. numpy — EXISTS. requests_oauth_helper — HALLUCINATED — 404. Slopsquatting risk flagged. The malicious payload would install on pip install without any warning.
We extend the scanner with a --npm flag for JavaScript imports using the npm registry API.

props.command:
claude "Add --npm flag:
  check npm registry API
  registry.npmjs.org/-/v1/search?text=<pkg>
  same EXISTS/HALLUCINATED output"

beats.narration_text: npm scan: react — EXISTS. lodash — EXISTS. react-query-optimizer-v3 — HALLUCINATED. The pattern is identical across ecosystems — any package name a model invents is a potential squatting target.
The hallucination rate is low but the recurrence rate is high — the same fake names appear across thousands of outputs. Scan imports before pip install, not after.
Next: write a five-artifact Software Design Document with Claude Code.
Musinique. Build it with a CLI. Then take it apart. At nikbearbrown.com.
